circular.title: Reserve Bank of India (Commercial Banks – Acquisition and Holding of Shares or Voting Rights) Directions, 2025
circular.summary: These Directions ensure that the ownership and control of Indian banking companies remain well-diversified and that major shareholders meet 'fit and proper' criteria on a continuing basis. Any person intending to acquire a 'major shareholding' must obtain prior approval from the Reserve Bank of India. The directions set strict shareholding limits for promoters and non-promoters, mandate a five-year lock-in period for significant acquisitions, and prohibit entities from FATF non-compliant jurisdictions from acquiring major stakes.

clauses.text: These directions shall be called the Reserve Bank of India (Commercial Banks – Acquisition and Holding of Shares or Voting Rights) Directions, 2025.
A banking company shall put in place a board-approved ‘fit and proper’ criterion for major shareholders.
clauses.summary: Obligation to establish fit and proper criteria

clauses.text: Any person intending an acquisition resulting in major shareholding must seek previous RBI approval through the PRAVAAH portal.
clauses.summary: Obligation to seek prior RBI approval

obligations.text: Obtain prior RBI approval before acquiring 5% or more of paid-up share capital or voting rights.
obligations.plain_english: Get RBI approval before buying significant shares

obligations.text: Put in place a board-approved ‘fit and proper’ criterion for major shareholders.

prohibitions.text: Persons from FATF non-compliant jurisdictions are prohibited from major shareholding.
prohibitions.plain_english: No major shareholding from non-compliant jurisdictions

penalties.amount: Under Section 12B(5) of B R Act, 1949
penalties.condition: Failure to obtain prior RBI approval

definitions.definition: Acquiring or agreeing to acquire shares (equity and preference) or voting rights, directly or indirectly.
Aggregate holding of 5% or more of the paid-up share capital or voting rights.
